16	
17	/*-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
18	 * Incremental collection of expired keys.
19	 *
20	 * When keys are accessed they are expired on-access. However we need a
21	 * mechanism in order to ensure keys are eventually removed when expired even
22	 * if no access is performed on them.
23	 *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
24	
25	/* Constants table from pow(0.98, 1) to pow(0.98, 16). 
26	 * Help calculating the db->avg_ttl. */
27	static double avg_ttl_factor[16] = {0.98, 0.9604, 0.941192, 0.922368, 0.903921, 0.885842, 0.868126, 0.850763, 0.833748, 0.817073, 0.800731, 0.784717, 0.769022, 0.753642, 0.738569, 0.723798};
28	
29	/* Helper function for the activeExpireCycle() function.
30	 * This function will try to expire the key-value entry that is stored in the 
31	 * hash table entry 'de' of the 'expires' hash table of a Redis database.
32	 *
33	 * If the key is found to be expired, it is removed from the database and
34	 * 1 is returned. Otherwise no operation is performed and 0 is returned.
35	 *
36	 * When a key is expired, server.stat_expiredkeys is incremented.
37	 *
38	 * The parameter 'now' is the current time in milliseconds as is passed
39	 * to the function to avoid too many gettimeofday() syscalls. */
40	int activeExpireCycleTryExpire(redisDb *db, kvobj *kv, long long now) {
41	    if (now < kvobjGetExpire(kv))
42	        return 0;
43	
44	    enterExecutionUnit(1, 0);
45	    sds key = kvobjGetKey(kv);
46	    robj *keyobj = createStringObject(key,sdslen(key));
47	    deleteExpiredKeyAndPropagate(db,keyobj);
48	    server.stat_expiredkeys_active++;
49	    decrRefCount(keyobj);
50	    exitExecutionUnit();
51	    /* Propagate the DEL command */
52	    postExecutionUnitOperations();
53	    return 1;
54	}
55	
56	/* Try to expire a few timed out keys. The algorithm used is adaptive and
57	 * will use few CPU cycles if there are few expiring keys, otherwise
58	 * it will get more aggressive to avoid that too much memory is used by
59	 * keys that can be removed from the keyspace.
60	 *
61	 * Every expire cycle tests multiple databases: the next call will start
62	 * again from the next db. No more than CRON_DBS_PER_CALL databases are
63	 * tested at every iteration.
64	 *
65	 * The function can perform more or less work, depending on the "type"
66	 * argument. It can execute a "fast cycle" or a "slow cycle". The slow
67	 * cycle is the main way we collect expired cycles: this happens with
68	 * the "server.hz" frequency (usually 10 hertz).
69	 *
70	 * However the slow cycle can exit for timeout, since it used too much time.
71	 * For this reason the function is also invoked to perform a fast cycle
72	 * at every event loop cycle, in the beforeSleep() function. The fast cycle
73	 * will try to perform less work, but will do it much more often.
74	 *
75	 * The following are the details of the two expire cycles and their stop
76	 * conditions:
77	 *
78	 * If type is ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_FAST the function will try to run a
79	 * "fast" expire cycle that takes no longer than ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_FAST_DURATION
80	 * microseconds, and is not repeated again before the same amount of time.
81	 * The cycle will also refuse to run at all if the latest slow cycle did not
82	 * terminate because of a time limit condition.
83	 *
84	 * If type is ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_SLOW, that normal expire cycle is
85	 * executed, where the time limit is a percentage of the REDIS_HZ period
86	 * as specified by the ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_SLOW_TIME_PERC define. In the
87	 * fast cycle, the check of every database is interrupted once the number
88	 * of already expired keys in the database is estimated to be lower than
89	 * a given percentage, in order to avoid doing too much work to gain too
90	 * little memory.
91	 *
92	 * The configured expire "effort" will modify the baseline parameters in
93	 * order to do more work in both the fast and slow expire cycles.
94	 */
95	
96	#define ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_KEYS_PER_LOOP 20 /* Keys for each DB loop. */
97	#define ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_FAST_DURATION 1000 /* Microseconds. */
98	#define ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_SLOW_TIME_PERC 25 /* Max % of CPU to use. */
99	#define ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_ACCEPTABLE_STALE 10 /* % of stale keys after which
100	                                                   we do extra efforts. */

1156	/* Replication buffer blocks is the list of replBufBlock.
1157	 *
1158	 * +--------------+       +--------------+       +--------------+
1159	 * | refcount = 1 |  ...  | refcount = 0 |  ...  | refcount = 2 |
1160	 * +--------------+       +--------------+       +--------------+
1161	 *      |                                            /       \
1162	 *      |                                           /         \
1163	 *      |                                          /           \
1164	 *  Repl Backlog                               Replica_A    Replica_B
1165	 *
1166	 * Each replica or replication backlog increments only the refcount of the
1167	 * 'ref_repl_buf_node' which it points to. So when replica walks to the next
1168	 * node, it should first increase the next node's refcount, and when we trim
1169	 * the replication buffer nodes, we remove node always from the head node which
1170	 * refcount is 0. If the refcount of the head node is not 0, we must stop
1171	 * trimming and never iterate the next node.
1172	 *
1173	 * For replicas in IO threads we don't update the refcount while sending the
1174	 * repl data, but only when the client is sent back to main. This avoids data
1175	 * races. In order to achieve this, the replicas keep track of following:
1176	 * - io_curr_repl_node - the current node we've reached.
1177	 * - io_bound_repl_node - the last node in the replication buffer as seen by
1178	 *                        the replica client before it was sent to IO thread
1179	 *
1180	 * When the client is sent to IO thread for the first time io_curr_repl_node is
1181	 * initialized with ref_repl_buf_node.
1182	 * When the client is sent back to main it can decrement ref_repl_buf_node's
1183	 * refcount and increment it for io_curr_repl_node, since all the nodes
1184	 * in-between are already sent and the client doesn't hold reference to them.
1185	 *
1186	 * `io_bound_repl_node` is needed because IO thread needs to know when to stop
1187	 * sending data. If it was reading directly from the replication buffer,
1188	 * there will be a data race, because main thread may be writing to it during
1189	 * `feedReplicationBuffer`. `io_bound_repl_node` is cached in the client
1190	 * together with its used size just before sending the client to IO thread
1191	 * in `enqueuePendingClienstToIOThreads`. */
1192

1197	    long long id;           /* The unique incremental number. */
1198	    long long repl_offset;  /* Start replication offset of the block. */
1199	    size_t size;            /* Capacity of the buf in bytes */
1200	    size_t used;            /* Count of written bytes */

18	 * Incremental collection of expired keys.
19	 *
20	 * When keys are accessed they are expired on-access. However we need a
21	 * mechanism in order to ensure keys are eventually removed when expired even
22	 * if no access is performed on them.

29	/* Helper function for the activeExpireCycle() function.
30	 * This function will try to expire the key-value entry that is stored in the 
31	 * hash table entry 'de' of the 'expires' hash table of a Redis database.
32	 *
33	 * If the key is found to be expired, it is removed from the database and
34	 * 1 is returned. Otherwise no operation is performed and 0 is returned.
35	 *
36	 * When a key is expired, server.stat_expiredkeys is incremented.
37	 *
38	 * The parameter 'now' is the current time in milliseconds as is passed
39	 * to the function to avoid too many gettimeofday() syscalls. */
40	int activeExpireCycleTryExpire(redisDb *db, kvobj *kv, long long now) {
41	    if (now < kvobjGetExpire(kv))
42	        return 0;
43	
44	    enterExecutionUnit(1, 0);
45	    sds key = kvobjGetKey(kv);
46	    robj *keyobj = createStringObject(key,sdslen(key));
47	    deleteExpiredKeyAndPropagate(db,keyobj);
48	    server.stat_expiredkeys_active++;
49	    decrRefCount(keyobj);
50	    exitExecutionUnit();
51	    /* Propagate the DEL command */
52	    postExecutionUnitOperations();
53	    return 1;
54	}

56	/* Try to expire a few timed out keys. The algorithm used is adaptive and
57	 * will use few CPU cycles if there are few expiring keys, otherwise
58	 * it will get more aggressive to avoid that too much memory is used by
59	 * keys that can be removed from the keyspace.
60	 *
61	 * Every expire cycle tests multiple databases: the next call will start
62	 * again from the next db. No more than CRON_DBS_PER_CALL databases are
63	 * tested at every iteration.
64	 *
65	 * The function can perform more or less work, depending on the "type"
66	 * argument. It can execute a "fast cycle" or a "slow cycle". The slow
67	 * cycle is the main way we collect expired cycles: this happens with
68	 * the "server.hz" frequency (usually 10 hertz).
69	 *
70	 * However the slow cycle can exit for timeout, since it used too much time.
71	 * For this reason the function is also invoked to perform a fast cycle
72	 * at every event loop cycle, in the beforeSleep() function. The fast cycle
73	 * will try to perform less work, but will do it much more often.
74	 *
75	 * The following are the details of the two expire cycles and their stop
76	 * conditions:
77	 *
78	 * If type is ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_FAST the function will try to run a
79	 * "fast" expire cycle that takes no longer than ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_FAST_DURATION
80	 * microseconds, and is not repeated again before the same amount of time.
81	 * The cycle will also refuse to run at all if the latest slow cycle did not
82	 * terminate because of a time limit condition.
83	 *
84	 * If type is ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_SLOW, that normal expire cycle is
85	 * executed, where the time limit is a percentage of the REDIS_HZ period
86	 * as specified by the ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_SLOW_TIME_PERC define. In the
87	 * fast cycle, the check of every database is interrupted once the number
88	 * of already expired keys in the database is estimated to be lower than
89	 * a given percentage, in order to avoid doing too much work to gain too
90	 * little memory.
91	 *
92	 * The configured expire "effort" will modify the baseline parameters in
93	 * order to do more work in both the fast and slow expire cycles.
94	 */
95	
96	#define ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_KEYS_PER_LOOP 20 /* Keys for each DB loop. */
97	#define ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_FAST_DURATION 1000 /* Microseconds. */
98	#define ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_SLOW_TIME_PERC 25 /* Max % of CPU to use. */
99	#define ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_ACCEPTABLE_STALE 10 /* % of stale keys after which
100	                                                   we do extra efforts. */
101	
102	#define HFE_DB_BASE_ACTIVE_EXPIRE_FIELDS_PER_SEC 10000
103	
104	/* Data used by the expire dict scan callback. */
105	typedef struct {
106	    redisDb *db;
107	    long long now;
108	    unsigned long sampled; /* num keys checked */
109	    unsigned long expired; /* num keys expired */
110	    long long ttl_sum; /* sum of ttl for key with ttl not yet expired */
111	    int ttl_samples; /* num keys with ttl not yet expired */
112	} expireScanData;
113	
114	void expireScanCallback(void *privdata, const dictEntry *de, dictEntryLink plink) {
115	    UNUSED(plink);
116	    kvobj *kv = dictGetKV(de);
117	    expireScanData *data = privdata;
118	    long long ttl  = kvobjGetExpire(kv) - data->now;
119	    if (activeExpireCycleTryExpire(data->db, kv, data->now)) {
120	        data->expired++;
121	    }
122	    if (ttl > 0) {
123	        /* We want the average TTL of keys yet not expired. */
124	        data->ttl_sum += ttl;

### **1. SETTING A TTL - Complete Call Path**

#### **A. Command Entry Points (expire.c)**

- Calls: `expireGenericCommand(c, commandTimeSnapshot(), UNIT_SECONDS)`

**expireatCommand** → expire.c:851-852

**pexpireCommand** → expire.c:856-857

**pexpireatCommand** → expire.c:861-862

#### **B. Core TTL Setting Function (expire.c:726-843)**

**expireGenericCommand** → expire.c:726-843
- Line 757: Calls `lookupKeyWrite(c->db, key)` to get kvobj
- Lines 807-820: If key already expired → calls `dbGenericDelete()` with `DB_FLAG_KEY_EXPIRED` (db.c:844)
- Line 822: If not already expired → calls `setExpire(c, c->db, key, when)` (db.c:2706)
- Lines 826-835: Rewrites command to PEXPIREAT for replication
- Line 838: Calls `notifyKeyspaceEvent(NOTIFY_GENERIC, "expire", key, c->db->id)`

#### **C. setExpire & setExpireByLink (db.c:2706-2755)**

**setExpire** → db.c:2706-2708
```c
kvobj *setExpire(client *c, redisDb *db, robj *key, long long when) {
    return setExpireByLink(c, db, key->ptr, when, NULL);
}
```

**setExpireByLink** → db.c:2711-2755
- Line 2713: Gets `slot = getKeySlot(key)`
- Line 2716-2717: If no keyLink provided, finds link: `kvstoreDictFindLink(db->keys, slot, key, NULL)`
- Line 2720: Gets kvobj from link: `kvobjGetExpire(kv)` checks for old expire
- Lines 2722-2725: **If old expire exists** → simply updates expire via `kvobjSetExpire(kv, when)`

#### **E. PERSIST Command - TTL Removal (expire.c:911-926)**

**persistCommand** → expire.c:911-926
- Line 913: Calls `lookupKeyWrite(c->db, c->argv[1])`
- Line 914: Calls `removeExpire(c->db, c->argv[1])` (db.c:2685)

**removeExpire** → db.c:2685-2697

- Line 2688: Finds entry in expires dict via `kvstoreDictTwoPhaseUnlinkFind(db->expires, slot, key->ptr, &table)`

#### **F. db->expires Dictionary Structure (server.h:1207-1222)**

```c
typedef struct redisDb {
    kvstore *keys;              /* main keyspace (line 1208) */
    kvstore *expires;           /* TIMEOUT OF KEYS (line 1209) - stores expiration times */
    estore *subexpires;         /* sub-key timeouts for hashes (line 1210) */
```

**Key Points:**
- `db->expires` is a `kvstore` (slot-based hash table)
- Stores same kvobj entries as `db->keys`, but with expiration time
- Keys stored as SDS strings, values store kvobj with embedded expiration
- Type: `kvstore *expires` (line 1209)

### **2. LAZY EXPIRY - Expiration on Access**

    ```c
        data.expired = 0;
        
        // Line 431: kvstoreScan calls expireScanCallback for each expired key
        db->expires_cursor = kvstoreScan(db->expires, db->expires_cursor, -1,
    ```

#### **C. expireScanCallback (expire.c:114-128)**

**expireScanCallback** → expire.c:114-128
```c
void expireScanCallback(void *privdata, const dictEntry *de, dictEntryLink plink) {
    kvobj *kv = dictGetKV(de);
    expireScanData *data = privdata;
    long long ttl = kvobjGetExpire(kv) - data->now;
    
    if (activeExpireCycleTryExpire(data->db, kv, data->now)) {  // line 119
        data->expired++;
    }
    if (ttl > 0) {
        data->ttl_sum += ttl;
        data->ttl_samples++;
    }
    data->sampled++;

```

#### **D. activeExpireCycleTryExpire (expire.c:40-54)**

**activeExpireCycleTryExpire** → expire.c:40-54
```c
int activeExpireCycleTryExpire(redisDb *db, kvobj *kv, long long now) {
    if (now < kvobjGetExpire(kv))  // line 41
        return 0;  /* Not expired */
    
    enterExecutionUnit(1, 0);  // line 44 - transaction-like boundary
    sds key = kvobjGetKey(kv);
    robj *keyobj = createStringObject(key, sdslen(key));
    
    deleteExpiredKeyAndPropagate(db, keyobj);  // line 47 - SAME AS LAZY EXPIRY!
    server.stat_expiredkeys_active++;
    decrRefCount(keyobj);
    exitExecutionUnit();
    postExecutionUnitOperations();  // line 52 - propagate DEL/UNLINK
    return 1;
}
```

**Key Insight:** Active expiry calls the exact same deletion function as lazy expiry:
- `deleteExpiredKeyAndPropagate(db, keyobj)` → calls `propagateDeletion()` → AOF/replication

#### **E. activeSubexpiresCycle (expire.c:228-285)**

**activeSubexpiresCycle** → expire.c:228-285
- Handles hash field expiration via ebuckets data structure
- Iterates through `db->subexpires` slots
- Calls `activeSubexpires(db, currentSlot, maxFieldsToExpire)` (expire.c:208-221)
- Which invokes `estoreActiveExpire(db->subexpires, slot, &info)` with callback `activeSubexpiresCb`

#### **F. expiryData and Statistics (expire.c:104-128)**

```c
typedef struct {
    redisDb *db;
    long long now;
    unsigned long sampled;  /* num keys checked */
    unsigned long expired;  /* num keys expired */
    long long ttl_sum;      /* sum of ttl for not-yet-expired */
    int ttl_samples;        /* num keys with ttl not yet expired */
} expireScanData;
```

- Line 405: `data.now = mstime()` - snapshot time for consistency
- Line 431: `kvstoreScan()` iterates db->expires dictionary
- Lines 442-480: Updates `db->avg_ttl` via exponential moving average
- Lines 452-489: Checks time limit every 16 iterations
- Lines 494-506: Updates global stats

---

### **4. REPLICA & CLUSTER MODE DIFFERENCES**

#### **A. Replica Key Expiration (expire.c:548-597)**

**expireSlaveKeys** → expire.c:548-597
- Tracks keys with expiry that were created on writable slaves
- Called periodically to expire writable-slave keys
- Uses `slaveKeysWithExpire` dictionary (expire.c:544)
- Iterates and calls `activeExpireCycleTryExpire()` for each key

**rememberSlaveKeyWithExpire** → expire.c:601-629
- Called during `setExpireByLink()` (line 2752-2753) if writable slave
- Adds key to `slaveKeysWithExpire` tracking dict with bitmask of DBs

#### **B. Cluster Mode Slot Management**

**expirySamplingShouldSkipDict** → expire.c:130-145
- Skips dicts (slots) during ASM (atomic slot migration)
- Checks `clusterCanAccessKeysInSlot(didx)` (line 142)

**Replication of Expiry:**
- DEL/UNLINK commands propagated via `propagateDeletion()` (db.c:2854)
- Uses `alsoPropagate()` with `PROPAGATE_AOF|PROPAGATE_REPL` flags (line 2866)
- Master-driven on replicas (read-only replicas don't delete, wait for master DEL)

---

### **5. CHECKSUM & FLAGS SUMMARY**

Now I have comprehensive data. Let me write a complete walkthrough of the Redis expiration subsystem:

## Complete Line-Numbered Walkthrough of Redis Key Expiration Subsystem

Based on my thorough reading of the Redis source code, here is a precise, line-numbered walkthrough of the three expiration paths:

---

### **1. SETTING A TTL - Complete Call Path**

#### **A. Command Entry Points (expire.c)**

**expireCommand** → expire.c:846-847
- Function signature: `void expireCommand(client *c)`
- Calls: `expireGenericCommand(c, commandTimeSnapshot(), UNIT_SECONDS)`

**expireatCommand** → expire.c:851-852
- Calls: `expireGenericCommand(c, 0, UNIT_SECONDS)`

**pexpireCommand** → expire.c:856-857
- Calls: `expireGenericCommand(c, commandTimeSnapshot(), UNIT_MILLISECONDS)`

**pexpireatCommand** → expire.c:861-862
- Calls: `expireGenericCommand(c, 0, UNIT_MILLISECONDS)`

#### **B. Core TTL Setting Function (expire.c:726-843)**

**expireGenericCommand** → expire.c:726-843
- Line 757: Calls `lookupKeyWrite(c->db, key)` to get kvobj
- Lines 807-820: If key already expired → calls `dbGenericDelete()` with `DB_FLAG_KEY_EXPIRED` (db.c:844)
- Line 822: If not already expired → calls `setExpire(c, c->db, key, when)` (db.c:2706)
- Lines 826-835: Rewrites command to PEXPIREAT for replication
- Line 838: Calls `notifyKeyspaceEvent(NOTIFY_GENERIC, "expire", key, c->db->id)`

#### **C. setExpire & setExpireByLink (db.c:2706-2755)**

**setExpire** → db.c:2706-2708
```c
kvobj *setExpire(client *c, redisDb *db, robj *key, long long when) {
    return setExpireByLink(c, db, key->ptr, when, NULL);
}
```

**setExpireByLink** → db.c:2711-2755
- Line 2713: Gets `slot = getKeySlot(key)`
- Line 2716-2717: If no keyLink provided, finds link: `kvstoreDictFindLink(db->keys, slot, key, NULL)`
- Line 2720: Gets kvobj from link: `kvobjGetExpire(kv)` checks for old expire
- Lines 2722-2725: **If old expire exists** → simply updates expire via `kvobjSetExpire(kv, when)`
- Lines 2726-2748: **If no old expire**:
  - Line 2732-2733: If hash with HFE, removes from `db->subexpires` via `estoreRemove()`
  - Line 2735: Calls `kvobjSetExpire(kv, when)` (may reallocate kvobj)
  - Lines 2737-2742: If reallocated, updates dict via `kvstoreDictSetAtLink()` and memory tracking
  - Line 2744: Adds entry to `db->expires` dict via `kvstoreDictAddRaw(db->expires, slot, kv, NULL)`
  - Line 2747-2748: If HFE, re-adds to subexpires via `estoreAdd()`
- Lines 2751-2753: If writable slave, calls `rememberSlaveKeyWithExpire(db, key)` (expire.c:601-629)

#### **D. SET with EX/PX/EXAT/PXAT Options (t_string.c:87-229)**

**setGenericCommand** → t_string.c:87-229
- Lines 95-97: Parses expire argument via `getExpireMillisecondsOrReply()`
- Line 104: Calls `lookupKeyWriteWithLink(c->db, key, &link)`
- Lines 161-175: If expire already elapsed, calls `dbDelete()` instead
- Line 178: Sets `SETKEY_KEEPTTL` flag if expire or KEEPTTL present
- Line 181: Calls `setKeyByLink(c, c->db, key, valref, setkey_flags, &link)` (db.c:754)
- Line 184: **Calls `setExpireByLink(c, c->db, key->ptr, milliseconds, link)`** (db.c:2711)
- Line 209: Calls `notifyKeyspaceEvent(NOTIFY_GENERIC, "expire", key, c->db->id)`

#### **E. PERSIST Command - TTL Removal (expire.c:911-926)**

**persistCommand** → expire.c:911-926
- Line 913: Calls `lookupKeyWrite(c->db, c->argv[1])`
- Line 914: Calls `removeExpire(c->db, c->argv[1])` (db.c:2685)

**removeExpire** → db.c:2685-2697
- Line 2687: Gets `slot = getKeySlot(key->ptr)`
- Line 2688: Finds entry in expires dict via `kvstoreDictTwoPhaseUnlinkFind(db->expires, slot, key->ptr, &table)`
- Line 2693: Sets expire to -1 via `kvobjSetExpire(kv, -1)`
- Line 2695: Removes from expires dict via `kvstoreDictTwoPhaseUnlinkFree()`

#### **F. db->expires Dictionary Structure (server.h:1207-1222)**

```c
typedef struct redisDb {
    kvstore *keys;              /* main keyspace (line 1208) */
    kvstore *expires;           /* TIMEOUT OF KEYS (line 1209) - stores expiration times */
    estore *subexpires;         /* sub-key timeouts for hashes (line 1210) */
    dict *blocking_keys;        /* blocked clients waiting data */
    dict *blocking_keys_unblock_on_nokey;
    dict *stream_claim_pending_keys;
    dict *stream_idmp_keys;
    dict *ready_keys;
    dict *watched_keys;
    int id;
    long long avg_ttl;          /* stats (line 1220) */
    unsigned long expires_cursor; /* active expire cycle cursor (line 1221) */
} redisDb;
```

**Key Points:**
- `db->expires` is a `kvstore` (slot-based hash table)
- Stores same kvobj entries as `db->keys`, but with expiration time
- Keys stored as SDS strings, values store kvobj with embedded expiration
- Type: `kvstore *expires` (line 1209)

---

### **2. LAZY EXPIRY - Expiration on Access**

#### **A. Entry Points - Key Lookup (db.c:279-352)**

**lookupKeyRead** → db.c:356-357
```c
kvobj *lookupKeyRead(redisDb *db, robj *key) {
    return lookupKeyReadWithFlags(db, key, LOOKUP_NONE);
}
```

**lookupKeyReadWithFlags** → db.c:349-351
```c
kvobj *lookupKeyReadWithFlags(redisDb *db, robj *key, int flags) {
    serverAssert(!(flags & LOOKUP_WRITE));
    return lookupKey(db, key, flags, NULL);
}
```

**lookupKeyWrite** → db.c:370-371
```c

}
```

```c
}
```

**lookupKey** → db.c:279-338

- Lines 283-306: If found:
  - Line 294: Sets `EXPIRE_FORCE_DELETE_EXPIRED` if LOOKUP_WRITE and not read-only replica
  - Line 296: Sets `EXPIRE_AVOID_DELETE_EXPIRED` if LOOKUP_NOEXPIRE
  - Lines 298-301: Sets `EXPIRE_ALLOW_ACCESS_EXPIRED`/`EXPIRE_ALLOW_ACCESS_TRIMMED` flags
  - **Line 302: Calls `expireIfNeeded(db, key, val, expire_flags)`** (db.c:2935)
  - Lines 303-306: If expired/deleted, sets val=NULL and returns NULL
- Lines 309-324: If still valid, updates LFU/LRU timestamp
- Lines 326-335: Updates hit/miss stats and sends keyspace events

#### **C. expireIfNeeded - Lazy Expiration Check (db.c:2935-3004)**

**expireIfNeeded** → db.c:2935-3004
```c
keyStatus expireIfNeeded(redisDb *db, robj *key, kvobj *kv, int flags)
```

**Logic Flow:**
- Line 2941: Gets key name from key or kvobj
- Lines 2942-2950: Checks if key is in trim job (cluster), returns KEY_TRIMMED
- Lines 2952-2954: Calls `keyIsExpired(db, key_name, kv)` (db.c:2877)
  - If not expired OR EXPIRE_ALLOW_ACCESS_EXPIRED set, returns KEY_VALID
- Lines 2974-2977: On replicas without EXPIRE_FORCE_DELETE_EXPIRED, returns KEY_EXPIRED (not deleted)
- Lines 2981-2982: If lazy-expire disabled in nested commands, returns KEY_EXPIRED
- Lines 2986-2987: If EXPIRE_AVOID_DELETE_EXPIRED set, returns KEY_EXPIRED
- Lines 2992: If expire action paused, returns KEY_EXPIRED
- **Lines 2995-3002: ACTUAL DELETION** →
  - If key provided: calls `deleteExpiredKeyAndPropagate(db, key)` (db.c:2826)
  - Else: creates temporary string object and deletes via same function
  - Returns KEY_DELETED

#### **D. keyIsExpired Helper (db.c:2877-2886)**

**keyIsExpired** → db.c:2877-2886
```c
int keyIsExpired(redisDb *db, sds key, kvobj *kv) {
    if (server.loading || server.allow_access_expired) return 0;
    mstime_t when = getExpire(db, key, kv);      // line 2880
    if (when < 0) return 0;  /* No expire for this key */
    const mstime_t now = commandTimeSnapshot();  // line 2882
    return now > when;
}
```

**getExpire** → db.c:2762-2766
```c
long long getExpire(redisDb *db, sds key, kvobj *kv) {
    if (kv == NULL) kv = dbFindExpires(db, key);
    if (kv == NULL) return -1;
    return kvobjGetExpire(kv);  /* retrieves embedded expiration */
}
```

**dbFindExpires** → db.c:3076-3077
```c
kvobj *dbFindExpires(redisDb *db, sds key) {
    return dbFindGeneric(db->expires, key);  /* looks up in expires dict */
}
```

#### **E. deleteExpiredKeyAndPropagate (db.c:2826-2828)**

**deleteExpiredKeyAndPropagate** → db.c:2826-2828
```c
void deleteExpiredKeyAndPropagate(redisDb *db, robj *keyobj) {
    deleteKeyAndPropagate(db, keyobj, NOTIFY_EXPIRED, NULL);
}
```

**deleteKeyAndPropagate** → db.c:2775-2823
- Line 2807: Calls `dbGenericDelete(db, keyobj, lazy_flag, del_flag)` (db.c:844)
  - lazy_flag = `server.lazyfree_lazy_expire` (controlled by config)
  - del_flag = `DB_FLAG_KEY_EXPIRED`
- Line 2812: Calls `notifyKeyspaceEvent(NOTIFY_EXPIRED, "expired", keyobj, db->id)`

- Line 2814: **Calls `propagateDeletion(db, keyobj, lazy_flag)`** (db.c:2854)
- Line 2817: Increments `server.stat_expiredkeys++`

#### **F. dbGenericDelete (db.c:844-898)**

**dbGenericDelete** → db.c:844-898
- Line 848: Two-phase unlink find: `kvstoreDictTwoPhaseUnlinkFind(db->keys, slot, key->ptr, &table)`
- Lines 851-858: If hash with expiry on fields, removes from `db->subexpires` via `estoreRemove()`
- Line 881-882: **If expirable, deletes from expires dict:**
  ```c
  if (kvobjGetExpire(kv) != -1)
      kvstoreDictDelete(db->expires, slot, key->ptr);
  ```
- Lines 884-890: If async, marks key as NULL and calls `freeObjAsync()`, else free immediately
- Line 891: Removes from main dict via `kvstoreDictTwoPhaseUnlinkFree()`

#### **G. propagateDeletion - AOF/Replication (db.c:2854-2871)**

**propagateDeletion** → db.c:2854-2871
```c
void propagateDeletion(redisDb *db, robj *key, int lazy) {
    robj *argv[2];
    argv[0] = lazy ? shared.unlink : shared.del;
    argv[1] = key;
    incrRefCount(argv[0]);
    incrRefCount(argv[1]);
    
    int prev_replication_allowed = server.replication_allowed;
    server.replication_allowed = 1;
    alsoPropagate(db->id, argv, 2, PROPAGATE_AOF|PROPAGATE_REPL);  // line 2866
    server.replication_allowed = prev_replication_allowed;
    
    decrRefCount(argv[0]);
    decrRefCount(argv[1]);
}
```
- **Line 2866: Propagates DEL or UNLINK to AOF and replicas**

---

### **3. ACTIVE EXPIRY - Background Expiration Cycle**

#### **A. serverCron Invocation → activeExpireCycle (expire.c:287-507)**

**serverCron** (server.c - main event loop, typically 10-100 Hz based on server.hz)
- Calls `activeExpireCycle(ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_SLOW)` periodically
- Calls `activeExpireCycle(ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_FAST)` in beforeSleep hook

#### **B. activeExpireCycle Main Function (expire.c:287-507)**

**activeExpireCycle** → expire.c:287-507
- Line 292: `effort = server.active_expire_effort - 1` (1-10 configurable)
- Lines 293-300: Scales parameters (keys_per_loop, fast_duration, slow_time_perc) based on effort
- Lines 304-306: Maintains static state: `current_db`, `timelimit_exit`, `last_fast_cycle`
- Lines 316: If expire action paused, returns immediately
- Lines 318-331: Fast cycle logic (limited duration, skipped if slow cycle timed out)
- Lines 340-341: Sets dbs_per_call based on dbnum
- Line 347: Calculates time limit in microseconds
- Line 361: Assertion `server.also_propagate.numops == 0`
- Lines 368-491: **Main loop over databases:**
  ```c
  for (j = 0; dbs_performed < dbs_per_call && timelimit_exit == 0 && j < server.dbnum; j++)
  ```
  - Line 374: Gets `redisDb *db = server.db + (current_db % server.dbnum)`
  - Line 383: Increments current_db
  - **Line 387: Calls `activeSubexpiresCycle(type)`** for hash field expiration
  - Lines 389-390: Only counts performed if db has expires
  - **Lines 396-491: Inner loop - scans expires with callback:**
    ```c
    do {
        // ... scan parameters ...
        data.now = mstime();  // line 405
        data.sampled = 0;
        data.expired = 0;
        
        // Line 431: kvstoreScan calls expireScanCallback for each expired key
        db->expires_cursor = kvstoreScan(db->expires, db->expires_cursor, -1,
                                        expireScanCallback,
                                        expirySamplingShouldSkipDict, &data);
        // ... continue until cursor == 0 or time limit ...
    } while (repeat);
    ```

#### **C. expireScanCallback (expire.c:114-128)**

**expireScanCallback** → expire.c:114-128
```c
void expireScanCallback(void *privdata, const dictEntry *de, dictEntryLink plink) {
    kvobj *kv = dictGetKV(de);
    expireScanData *data = privdata;
    long long ttl = kvobjGetExpire(kv) - data->now;
    
    if (activeExpireCycleTryExpire(data->db, kv, data->now)) {  // line 119
        data->expired++;
    }
    if (ttl > 0) {
        data->ttl_sum += ttl;
        data->ttl_samples++;
    }
    data->sampled++;
}
```

#### **D. activeExpireCycleTryExpire (expire.c:40-54)**

**activeExpireCycleTryExpire** → expire.c:40-54
```c
int activeExpireCycleTryExpire(redisDb *db, kvobj *kv, long long now) {
    if (now < kvobjGetExpire(kv))  // line 41
        return 0;  /* Not expired */
    
    enterExecutionUnit(1, 0);  // line 44 - transaction-like boundary
    sds key = kvobjGetKey(kv);
    robj *keyobj = createStringObject(key, sdslen(key));
    
    deleteExpiredKeyAndPropagate(db, keyobj);  // line 47 - SAME AS LAZY EXPIRY!
    server.stat_expiredkeys_active++;
    decrRefCount(keyobj);
    exitExecutionUnit();
    postExecutionUnitOperations();  // line 52 - propagate DEL/UNLINK
    return 1;
}
```

**Key Insight:** Active expiry calls the exact same deletion function as lazy expiry:
- `deleteExpiredKeyAndPropagate(db, keyobj)` → calls `propagateDeletion()` → AOF/replication

#### **E. activeSubexpiresCycle (expire.c:228-285)**

**activeSubexpiresCycle** → expire.c:228-285
- Handles hash field expiration via ebuckets data structure
- Iterates through `db->subexpires` slots
- Calls `activeSubexpires(db, currentSlot, maxFieldsToExpire)` (expire.c:208-221)
- Which invokes `estoreActiveExpire(db->subexpires, slot, &info)` with callback `activeSubexpiresCb`

#### **F. expiryData and Statistics (expire.c:104-128)**

```c
typedef struct {
    redisDb *db;
    long long now;
    unsigned long sampled;  /* num keys checked */
    unsigned long expired;  /* num keys expired */
    long long ttl_sum;      /* sum of ttl for not-yet-expired */
    int ttl_samples;        /* num keys with ttl not yet expired */
} expireScanData;
```

- Line 405: `data.now = mstime()` - snapshot time for consistency
- Line 431: `kvstoreScan()` iterates db->expires dictionary

```
  └─ setGenericCommand    t_string.c:87
      ├─ lookupKeyWriteWithLink  db.c:380
      ├─ setKeyByLink     db.c:754          (inserts/replaces value)
      └─ setExpireByLink  db.c:2711         (same function as above)
```

**Key invariant:** the expiry deadline lives inside the `kvobj` itself. `db->expires` is an index that lets the active-expiry scanner enumerate only keys that have a TTL, without scanning all of `db->keys`.

---

### Path 2 — Lazy Expiry (on key access)

Every read or write command goes through `lookupKey`. Expiry is checked synchronously on the access thread before the command sees the value.

```
GET (or any command doing a key lookup)
  └─ lookupKeyRead        db.c:356
      └─ lookupKeyReadWithFlags  db.c:349
          └─ lookupKey    db.c:279
              ├─ dbFindByLink(db, key->ptr, link)   find kvobj in db->keys
              ├─ [if found] build expire_flags:
              │   EXPIRE_FORCE_DELETE_EXPIRED   if LOOKUP_WRITE && not read-only replica  db.c:294
              │   EXPIRE_AVOID_DELETE_EXPIRED   if LOOKUP_NOEXPIRE                         db.c:296
              │   EXPIRE_ALLOW_ACCESS_EXPIRED   if LOOKUP_ACCESS_EXPIRED                   db.c:298
              └─ expireIfNeeded(db, key, val, expire_flags)  db.c:2935   ← core gate

expireIfNeeded          db.c:2935   returns KEY_VALID | KEY_EXPIRED | KEY_DELETED | KEY_TRIMMED
  ├─ asmIsKeyInTrimJob(key_name)       db.c:2942   (cluster slot migration guard)
  ├─ EXPIRE_ALLOW_ACCESS_EXPIRED set?  db.c:2952   → KEY_VALID (caller wants the expired value)
  ├─ keyIsExpired(db, key, kv)         db.c:2877
  │   ├─ server.loading || allow_access_expired → 0 (never expire during load)
  │   ├─ getExpire(db, key, kv)        db.c:2762   → kvobjGetExpire(kv) (or lookup in db->expires)
  │   └─ return commandTimeSnapshot() > when
  ├─ [not expired] → KEY_VALID
  ├─ [replica, no FORCE flag] → KEY_EXPIRED (report expired but do not delete; wait for master DEL)
  ├─ confAllowsExpireDel()             db.c:2889   (nested-cmd / arbitrary-keys guard)
  ├─ EXPIRE_AVOID_DELETE_EXPIRED set?  → KEY_EXPIRED
  ├─ isPausedActionsWithUpdate(PAUSE_ACTION_EXPIRE) → KEY_EXPIRED
  └─ [master, delete allowed]
      └─ deleteExpiredKeyAndPropagate(db, key)  db.c:2826
          └─ deleteKeyAndPropagate(db, key, NOTIFY_EXPIRED, NULL)  db.c:2775
              ├─ lazy_flag = server.lazyfree_lazy_expire
              ├─ dbGenericDelete(db, key, lazy_flag, DB_FLAG_KEY_EXPIRED)  db.c:844
              │   ├─ kvstoreDictTwoPhaseUnlinkFind(db->keys, slot, key)     unlink from main dict
              │   ├─ [OBJ_HASH] estoreRemove(db->subexpires, slot, kv)      unhook HFE
              │   ├─ kvobjGetExpire(kv) != -1 →
              │   │   kvstoreDictDelete(db->expires, slot, key)              remove from expires dict
              │   ├─ [async] freeObjAsync(key, kv, db->id)                  lazy-free via BIO thread
              │   └─ kvstoreDictTwoPhaseUnlinkFree(db->keys, ...)            complete removal
              ├─ notifyKeyspaceEvent(NOTIFY_EXPIRED, "expired", ...)
              ├─ keyModified(NULL, db, key, NULL, 1)                         CSC invalidation
              ├─ propagateDeletion(db, key, lazy_flag)  db.c:2854
              │   └─ alsoPropagate(db->id, [DEL|UNLINK, key], 2,
              │                    PROPAGATE_AOF|PROPAGATE_REPL)             db.c:2866
              └─ server.stat_expiredkeys++
      return KEY_DELETED
```

Back in `lookupKey` (db.c:302–306): if `expireIfNeeded` returns anything other than `KEY_VALID`, `val` is set to `NULL` and the lookup returns `NULL`. The command then sees a missing key.

### Path 3 — Active Expiry (background, server cron)

Two call sites in the event loop:
- **Slow cycle**: `serverCron` → `activeExpireCycle(ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_SLOW)` — runs at `server.hz` (default 10 Hz), may use up to `slow_time_perc`% of one CPU cycle
- **Fast cycle**: `beforeSleep` → `activeExpireCycle(ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_FAST)` — short bursts capped to `config_cycle_fast_duration` µs, skipped if the previous slow cycle did not hit the time limit

```
activeExpireCycle(type)    expire.c:287
  ├─ isPausedActionsWithUpdate(PAUSE_ACTION_EXPIRE)  → return immediately
  ├─ [FAST cycle guards]     expire.c:318-331  (skip if not needed, or too recent)
  ├─ timelimit = slow_time_perc*1000000/server.hz/100   (µs budget per call)
  └─ for each DB (up to dbs_per_call, respecting timelimit):
      ├─ activeSubexpiresCycle(type)    expire.c:228    ← hash-field (HFE) expiry
      │   └─ activeSubexpires(db, slot, maxFieldsToExpire)  expire.c:208
      │       └─ estoreActiveExpire(db->subexpires, slot, &info)
      │           └─ [for each expired field] activeSubexpiresCb  expire.c:170
      │               └─ hashTypeExpire(db, kv, &quota, 0, 1)
      │
      └─ do { ... } while (expired/sampled > acceptable_stale && !timelimit_exit)
          ├─ data.now = mstime()                         expire.c:405  (snapshot for the scan batch)
          └─ kvstoreScan(db->expires, db->expires_cursor, -1,
                         expireScanCallback,
                         expirySamplingShouldSkipDict,
                         &data)                          expire.c:431
              │  cursor saved in db->expires_cursor — scan resumes across calls
              │
              ├─ expirySamplingShouldSkipDict  expire.c:130
              │   ├─ skip if dict is <1% full (avoid sparse scans)
              │   └─ skip if clusterCanAccessKeysInSlot() == 0 (slot migration)
              │
              └─ expireScanCallback(privdata, de, plink)  expire.c:114
                  ├─ kv = dictGetKV(de)
                  ├─ ttl = kvobjGetExpire(kv) - data.now
                  └─ activeExpireCycleTryExpire(data->db, kv, data->now)  expire.c:40
                      ├─ now < kvobjGetExpire(kv) → return 0  (not yet expired)
                      └─ [expired]:
                          ├─ enterExecutionUnit(1, 0)
                          ├─ keyobj = createStringObject(kvobjGetKey(kv), ...)
                          ├─ deleteExpiredKeyAndPropagate(db, keyobj)   db.c:2826
                          │   └─ [identical to lazy path above]
                          ├─ server.stat_expiredkeys_active++
                          ├─ exitExecutionUnit()
                          └─ postExecutionUnitOperations()   ← flushes also_propagate queue
                                                              (AOF write + replication send)
```

**Why `postExecutionUnitOperations` matters here:** lazy expiry fires inside `call()`, which handles propagation itself. Active expiry fires outside `call()`, so each batch of deletions needs an explicit flush of the `also_propagate` queue. The `serverAssert(server.also_propagate.numops == 0)` at expire.c:361 checks the slate is clean at entry.

---

### Replica and Writable-Slave Details

| Scenario | `expireIfNeeded` behaviour |
|---|---|
| Read-only replica, no `FORCE` flag | Returns `KEY_EXPIRED` (lies to caller), does **not** delete. Waits for `DEL`/`UNLINK` from master via replication stream. |
| Client is `CLIENT_MASTER` (replication feed) | Returns `KEY_VALID` — never expire keys the master just sent. |
| Writable replica | `EXPIRE_FORCE_DELETE_EXPIRED` is set on write-path lookups; deletion + propagation proceeds normally. Key is also tracked in `slaveKeysWithExpire` (expire.c:544) so `expireSlaveKeys` (expire.c:548) can sweep them periodically. |

`propagateDeletion` (db.c:2854) always forces `server.replication_allowed = 1` before calling `alsoPropagate`, so expiry-driven `DEL`/`UNLINK` are propagated even inside module contexts that disabled replication.
